quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 7
quiz.subtitle: Refraction — Calculations
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question: The speed of light in a vacuum is $3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$. In glass, light travels at $2.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$. What is the refractive index of the glass?
Diamond has a refractive index of $2.42$. What is the speed of light in diamond? ($c = 3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$)
Light travels from air ($n_1 = 1.0$) into water ($n_2 = 1.33$) at an angle of incidence of $30°$. What is $\sin r$? ($\sin 30° = 0.50$)

Light traveling in air ($n = 1.0$) strikes a transparent block at an angle of $45°$. The angle of refraction is $28°$. What is the refractive index of the block? ($\sin 45° = 0.707$, $\sin 28° = 0.469$)
If the refractive index of a medium is $2.0$, the speed of light in that medium is:
options: Half the speed of light in a vacuum
Twice the speed of light in a vacuum
Equal to the speed of light in a vacuum
Four times the speed of light in a vacuum
questions.explanation: $v = c/n = c/2$. The speed is halved.

questions.question: Light travels from water ($n_1 = 1.33$) into air ($n_2 = 1.0$). The angle of refraction is $60°$. What is the angle of incidence? ($\sin 60° = 0.866$)
The refractive index of crown glass is $1.52$. Calculate the speed of light in crown glass. ($c = 3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$)
Light travels through an unknown liquid at $2.25 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$. What is the refractive index of the liquid? ($c = 3.0 \times 10^8\text{ m/s}$)
Light passes from medium A ($n = 1.5$) into medium B ($n = 1.5$) at an angle of $40°$. What is the angle of refraction?
questions.explanation: Equal refractive indices mean no change in speed and therefore no bending. $r = i = 40°$.

questions.question: Medium X has $n = 1.4$ and Medium Y has $n = 1.8$. In which medium does light travel faster?
questions.explanation: Lower refractive index means faster light speed. $n = c/v$, so smaller $n$ means larger $v$.

questions.question: Light passes from air ($n = 1.0$) into a medium with $n = 1.6$ at an angle of $50°$. Will the refracted ray bend toward or away from the normal?
questions.explanation: Light slows down in the denser medium (higher $n$), causing it to bend toward the normal.

questions.question: If light slows to one-third of its vacuum speed in a medium, what is the refractive index of that medium?
questions.explanation: $n = c/v = c/(c/3) = 3.0$. The refractive index is 3.0.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered Snell's Law and refractive index calculations!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review solving for angles using $n_1\sin i = n_2\sin r$.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $n = c/v$ and applying Snell's Law correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: refractive index formula, Snell's Law, and the relationship between $n$ and speed.
